headline: What Moved Markets This Week
summary: U.S. stocks ended the week mostly unchanged amid persistent inflation pressures and surging Treasury yields. Read more about this week's major events on Wall Street.

headline: Does NANO Nuclear Energy’s (NNE) Supermicro AI Pact Redefine Its Microreactor Commercialization Path?
summary: NANO Nuclear Energy Inc. has already reported its Q2 fiscal 2026 results, narrowing its quarterly net loss to US$9.18 million and six‑month loss to US$15.70 million, while also highlighting progress on its KRONOS MMR microreactor program and fuel‑cycle initiatives. In parallel, the company advanced a Construction Permit Application for its KRONOS MMR at the University of Illinois and entered a non‑binding Memorandum of Understanding with Super Micro Computer, Inc. to explore nuclear‑powered...

headline: Netlist Q1 Earnings Call Highlights
summary: Netlist (OTCMKTS:NLST) reported record quarterly revenue for the first quarter of 2026, citing strong demand for memory products, tight industry supply and higher DRAM pricing as key drivers of its performance. headline: Why Super Micro (SMCI) Stock Is Down Today
summary: Shares of server solutions provider Super Micro (NASDAQ:SMCI) fell 3.8% in the afternoon session after a Goldman Sachs analyst reiterated a Sell rating on the stock, citing significant risks, amid a broader slump in technology stocks.
